algorithms.title: Geriatric Fall Assessment Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for assessing a geriatric patient after a fall, considering both traumatic and underlying medical causes.
steps: Ask about the circumstances of the fall: witnessed vs. unwitnessed, any preceding symptoms (dizziness, palpitations, chest discomfort), and ability to recall the event.
Check vital signs and blood glucose to screen for a medical cause of the fall.
Review the patient's medication list, specifically asking about anticoagulant/antiplatelet use.
Perform a thorough injury exam, keeping in mind that older adults may have a blunted pain response masking occult injury.
If anticoagulant use is present with any head injury, treat as elevated intracranial hemorrhage risk regardless of the initial exam, and communicate this clearly at handoff.
If findings suggest a medical cause (arrhythmia, orthostatic hypotension, hypoglycemia, infection), evaluate and manage accordingly rather than treating the fall as purely mechanical.
